name: Phase 3: Stacking Reduction
summary:
Phase 3 takes the per-AIR column opening claims gathered at the end of batch-constraint (BC) and reduces them to a single multilinear claim that WHIR can verify. Six AIRs cooperate: OpeningClaimsAir batches all column openings into an initial sum s_0; UnivariateRoundAir runs one univariate round over the multiplicative subgroup D of size 2^l_skip; SumcheckRoundsAir runs the n_stack quadratic multilinear rounds; EqBaseAir and EqBitsAir precompute the eq-kernel evaluations that convert the final sumcheck claim into a coefficient vector over stacked columns; and StackingClaimsAir closes the sumcheck, samples the WHIR batching challenge mu (with proof-of-work), and emits the WHIR claim.

Handoff is strict on both sides. On entry, the transcript index tidx_stack is received on StackingModuleBus, column claims arrive on ColumnClaimsBus, and the BC sumcheck randomness r is received on ConstraintSumcheckRandomnessBus. On exit, the final whir_claim is sent on WhirModuleBus, the batching challenge on WhirMuBus, and the l_skip + n_stack opening-point coordinates (u_cube) on WhirOpeningPointBus.

blocks.html: Before any AIR fires, the preflight driver in stacking/mod.rs replays the exact verifier transcript sequence so that each AIR knows its starting tidx. Reading this once makes the per-AIR transcript constraints much easier to follow.

blocks.heading: Preflight: verifier transcript order
blocks.explain: The three intermediate_tidx checkpoints are the module-local handoff points between the four transcript-owning AIRs. StackingModuleTidxBus threads them: opening → univariate → sumcheck → claims. The PoW-and-sample pattern pow_observe_sample is crucial for mu: the witness is observed before sampling the challenge so that the sponge commits to the grinding effort.

blocks.heading: OpeningClaimsAir
blocks.html: OpeningClaimsAir is the entry point. It iterates the per-AIR column openings in sort order, observes each (col_claim, rot_claim) on the transcript, samples the batching scalar lambda, and emits two downstream artifacts: the initial sum s_0 (to UnivariateRoundAir) and a per-stacked-column coefficient (to StackingClaimsAir).

blocks.heading: OpeningClaimsCols: what each row holds
blocks.code:
#[repr(C)]
#[derive(AlignedBorrow)]
pub struct OpeningClaimsCols<F> {
    // Proof index columns for continuations
    pub proof_idx: F,
    pub is_valid: F,
    pub is_first: F,
    pub is_last: F,

    // Received from batch constraints module
    pub sort_idx: F,
    pub part_idx: F,
    pub col_idx: F,
    pub col_claim: [F; D_EF],
    pub rot_claim: [F; D_EF],
    pub need_rot: F,

    // Used to constrain the order of received messages
    pub is_main: F,
    pub is_transition_main: F,

    // From proof shape (n, n_lift + l_skip, 2^{n_lift + l_skip}, 2^{- (n_lift + l_skip)})
    pub hypercube_dim: F,
    pub log_lifted_height: F,
    pub lifted_height: F,
    pub lifted_height_inv: F,

    // Sampled transcript values
    pub tidx: F,
    pub lambda: [F; D_EF],
    pub lambda_pow: [F; D_EF],

    // Location in stacked matrices
    pub commit_idx: F,
    pub stacked_col_idx: F,
    pub row_idx: F,
    pub is_last_for_claim: F,

    // Lookups to compute claim coefficient
    pub eq_in: [F; D_EF],
    pub k_rot_in: [F; D_EF],
    pub eq_bits: [F; D_EF],

    // This is either `k_rot_in` or zero, depending on `need_rot`
    pub k_rot_in_when_needed: [F; D_EF],

    // Intermediate values to compute claim coefficient
    pub lambda_pow_eq_bits: [F; D_EF],

    // Stacking claim coefficient to be sent
    pub stacking_claim_coefficient: [F; D_EF],

    // RLC of column claims * coefficient using lambda
    pub s_0: [F; D_EF],
}
blocks.explain: Every row represents one column opening claim. (sort_idx, part_idx, col_idx) identify where the claim came from (which AIR and which commit partition); (commit_idx, stacked_col_idx, row_idx) identify where that column lives in the stacked matrix. s_0 is a running RLC accumulator; stacking_claim_coefficient is the per-stacked-column running coefficient for the downstream inner-product.

blocks.heading: Sortedness of incoming column claims
blocks.explain: Main-commit claims (part_idx = 0) must come first, ordered by (sort_idx, col_idx); then non-main claims ordered by (sort_idx, part_idx, col_idx). This sorting is load-bearing: the later running-coefficient update relies on claims for a single stacked column arriving contiguously.

blocks.heading: Forward claims to BC and gate rot_claim on need_rot
blocks.explain: Each claim is echoed back to ColumnClaimsBus so that BatchConstraintAir can pair it with the claim it computed internally. rot_claim is zero-forced on rows whose AIR does not need rotation (the need_rot flag itself is looked up from AirShapeBus, later in the AIR).

blocks.heading: Accumulate s_0 and forward to UnivariateRoundAir
blocks.explain: This is the s_0 accumulator: s_0 += lambda_pow * (col_claim + lambda * rot_claim), with lambda_pow squaring lambda each step to give lambda^{2i}. The final s_0 is sent on SumcheckClaimsBus under module_idx = 0, consumed by the univariate round.

blocks.heading: Per-stacked-column running coefficient
blocks.explain: Multiple original columns can hash into the same stacked column (different sort_idx but shared (commit_idx, stacked_col_idx)). Each contributes lambda_pow * eq_bits * (eq_in + lambda * k_rot_in_when_needed); the running sum resets on is_last_for_claim and is sent on ClaimCoefficientsBus for consumption by StackingClaimsAir.

blocks.heading: Eq-kernel and eq-bits lookups
blocks.explain: The three lookups deliver (a) the proof-shape fields (hypercube_dim, lifted_height) from LiftedHeightsBus, (b) the prism kernel values eq_in = eq_n(u, r) and k_rot_in = k_rot_n(u, r) from EqKernelLookupBus (produced by EqBaseAir/SumcheckRoundsAir), and (c) the eq-MLE value eq_bits = eq_{n_stack - n_j}(u_{>n_j}, b_j) from EqBitsLookupBus (produced by EqBitsAir).

blocks.heading: Transcript observations and handoff to UnivariateRoundAir
blocks.explain: Each valid row observes col_claim and rot_claim (2 * D_EF extension-field coordinates) on the transcript, advancing tidx. On the final row the AIR samples lambda (is_sample = 1) and forwards the post-sample tidx to UnivariateRoundAir via the internal StackingModuleTidxBus with module_idx = 0.

blocks.heading: UnivariateRoundAir
blocks.html: UnivariateRoundAir runs a single univariate sumcheck round over the multiplicative subgroup D of size |D| = 2^l_skip. It ingests the 2|D|-1 coefficients of the round polynomial s_0, checks sum_{z in D} s_0(z) == s_0_claim (expressed via the identity |D| * (a_0 + a_{|D|})), samples u_0, and evaluates s_0(u_0) via Horner.

blocks.heading: UnivariateRoundCols
blocks.explain: Each row carries one coefficient coeff = s_0[coeff_idx]. The index coeff_idx walks 0..2*(|D|-1). coeff_is_d flags coeff_idx == |D|, the single coefficient used in the sum-over-D identity.

blocks.heading: Sum-over-D identity
blocks.explain: Because D is a multiplicative subgroup, sum_{z in D} s_0(z) collapses to |D| * (a_0 + a_{|D|}). The AIR accumulates exactly those two coefficients into s_0_sum_over_d and receives it on SumcheckClaimsBus with module_idx = 0 — this pairs with the send from OpeningClaimsAir, forcing equality.

blocks.heading: Horner evaluation of s_0(u_0)
Forward u_0 on EqRandValuesLookupBus
blocks.explain: u_0 is used by multiple downstream consumers: EqBaseAir (for iterative squaring with r_0) and EqBitsAir (for the bit-tree node at idx = 0, implicitly; actually this is the extension-field value for idx = 0 in the eq-rand table). Multiplicity 2 budgets for both consumers.

blocks.heading: Transcript: coefficients, sample u_0, hand off to SumcheckRoundsAir
blocks.explain: Each row observes one coefficient (D_EF field elements) — total 2|D|-1 rows — then the last row samples u_0. The post-sample tidx is forwarded with module_idx = 1 to SumcheckRoundsAir.

blocks.heading: SumcheckRoundsAir
blocks.html: SumcheckRoundsAir runs the n_stack quadratic multilinear sumcheck rounds. Each row handles one round j ∈ {1, …, n_stack}: receive the current claim, observe s_j(1) and s_j(2) (with s_j(0) recovered from the claim), sample u_j, interpolate s_j(u_j), and send it on as the next claim. Simultaneously, the AIR maintains the running eq_cube and rot_cube products needed to convert the final claim into the eq-kernel lookups consumed by OpeningClaimsAir.

blocks.heading: SumcheckRoundsCols
Claim chain and quadratic interpolation
blocks.explain: Round 1 receives s_0(u_0) from UnivariateRoundAir (module_idx = 1) and verifies it equals s_1(0) + s_1(1). Each round fits a quadratic p*x^2 + q*x + s(0) through the three sampled evaluations, then evaluates at u_round. The final round sends s_{n_stack}(u_{n_stack}) on module_idx = 2, which StackingClaimsAir will close out.

blocks.heading: Receive eq-base from EqBaseAir
blocks.explain: The three base values — eq_0(u, r), eq_0(u, r*omega), and the product eq_0(u, 1) * eq_0(r*omega, 1) — arrive once per proof from EqBaseAir on EqBaseBus. They are copied unchanged down the rounds so each round can multiply them into its running eq_cube / rot_cube.

blocks.heading: Running eq_cube and rot_cube updates
blocks.explain: eq_cube accumulates the product form of eq(u_{>0}, r_{>0}) across rounds (each factor is 1 - u*(1-r) - r*(1-u)). r_not_u_prod is the prefix product of r*(1-u); rot_cube_minus_prod follows a recurrence that lets rot_cube = eq_cube - (r_not_u_prod + rot_cube_minus_prod) be reconstructed without any field inversions.

blocks.heading: Provide eq_n(u, r) lookup for rounds n = 1..n_stack
blocks.explain: For each round n = 1..n_stack this publishes an EqKernelLookupMessage so that OpeningClaimsAir can look up eq_in and k_rot_in at the right n. The has_r flag marks whether round n actually has a paired BC randomness (rounds with n > n_max do not, so they neither publish lookups nor consume r).

blocks.heading: Forward u_round on WhirOpeningPointBus (u_cube)
blocks.explain: This is the link to WHIR. Each u_round is sent on WhirOpeningPointBus at index round + l_skip - 1, filling entries l_skip..l_skip + n_stack of u_cube. Entries 0..l_skip come from EqBaseAir (the iterative squarings of u_0). The BC randomness r_round is received from ConstraintSumcheckRandomnessBus.

blocks.heading: Transcript: observe s_j(1), s_j(2); sample u_j
blocks.explain: Each round consumes 3 * D_EF transcript slots: s_j(1) and s_j(2) observed, then u_j sampled. The final row hands tidx off to StackingClaimsAir with module_idx = 2.

blocks.heading: EqBaseAir
blocks.html: EqBaseAir precomputes the eq-kernel base values at depth zero: eq_0(u, r), eq_0(u, r*omega), and the prefix products needed to reach them. It also produces u_pow = u_0^{2^i} for i = 0..l_skip-1 — the first l_skip entries of WHIR's u_cube — via iterative squaring.

blocks.heading: EqBaseCols
row_idx and u_cube handoff
blocks.explain: row_idx walks 0, 1, …, l_skip. For rows 0..l_skip it sends u_0^{2^row_idx} on WhirOpeningPointBus under index row_idx. Combined with SumcheckRoundsAir's contributions at indices l_skip..l_skip + n_stack, WHIR receives exactly l_skip + n_stack opening-point entries.

blocks.heading: Receive u_0 and r_0, export to EqNegAir
Iterative squaring: prod_u_r and prod_u_r_omega
blocks.explain: At each row u_pow and r_pow square to u^{2^{row+1}} and r^{2^{row+1}}. The running products prod_u_r and prod_u_r_omega tabulate ∏_{i=0}^{row} (u^{2^i} + r^{2^i}) and the analogous sum with r*omega. These are the classical building blocks for eq_0(u, r) (Lagrange-basis multilinear eval, collapsed into a product).

blocks.heading: Close eq_0 values and publish on EqKernelLookupBus / EqBaseBus
blocks.explain: On the last row the three base values are sealed: eq_0(u, r) and its omega-shift are added to the EqKernelLookupBus under n = 0; eq_0(u, 1) * eq_0(r*omega, 1) goes out on EqBaseBus so SumcheckRoundsAir can initialise rot_cube.

blocks.heading: Negative-n eq lookups (for AIRs with n_j < 0)
blocks.explain: For AIRs whose hypercube dimension is smaller than l_skip, n_j = log_height - l_skip is negative. The negative-n kernel values are computed externally in EqNegAir (shared with BC) and received here on EqNegResultBus. The in_prod column supplies the in_n(u) factor. All together these publish additional EqKernelLookupBus entries at n = -1, …, -l_skip.

blocks.heading: EqBitsAir
blocks.html: EqBitsAir evaluates eq_{n_stack - n_j}(u_{>n_j}, b_j) for every distinct (b_value, num_bits) that any stacked column needs. It is a binary-tree AIR: each row is one node, deriving its evaluation from its parent's via a single multiplicative factor determined by the LSB of b_value.

blocks.heading: EqBitsCols
Root node and parent lookup
Derive child evaluation from parent
blocks.explain: Each row's evaluation is sub_eval * ((1 - u) + b_lsb * (2u - 1)), i.e. sub_eval * u when b_lsb = 1 and sub_eval * (1 - u) when b_lsb = 0. This is the multilinear-eq recurrence applied one bit at a time. The b_value reconstruction 2 * parent + lsb extends the bit string MSB-first.

blocks.heading: Send children on EqBitsInternalBus (both LSBs)
blocks.explain: internal_child_flag is a compact encoding: its low bit signals a lsb = 0 child and its upper bit signals a lsb = 1 child. The cubic polynomials select which children's sends fire. The row's eval also feeds the external EqBitsLookupBus with multiplicity external_mult — that's what OpeningClaimsAir consumes.

blocks.heading: StackingClaimsAir
blocks.html: StackingClaimsAir closes the phase. It observes each stacked-column opening, receives the paired claim_coefficient from OpeningClaimsAir, and accumulates final_s_eval = Σ stacking_claim · claim_coefficient — which must equal s_{n_stack}(u_{n_stack}) received from SumcheckRoundsAir. It then runs the mu PoW, samples mu, and emits whir_claim as a mu-weighted RLC of the stacking claims.

blocks.heading: StackingClaimsCols
Block layout: valid + padding rows, w_stack boundary
blocks.explain: Each proof block has exactly w_stack rows. Valid rows (which fire bus interactions) come first; padding rows fill the block up to the fixed-size boundary. global_col_idx must end at w_stack - 1 — this is the VK-enforced shape check that ties the column count to the verifying key.

blocks.heading: Running sum final_s_eval = Σ stacking_claim · claim_coefficient
blocks.explain: Per row: receive claim_coefficient from OpeningClaimsAir, multiply by the observed stacking_claim, add to the running sum. The last valid row's final_s_eval is received on SumcheckClaimsBus under module_idx = 2 — pairing with the send from SumcheckRoundsAir, this closes the sumcheck chain: Σ_i claim_i · coeff_i = s_{n_stack}(u_{n_stack}).

blocks.heading: Observe stacking_claim and schedule tidx
blocks.explain: Each valid row consumes D_EF transcript slots to observe its stacking_claim, so after w_stack valid rows the tidx advances by w_stack * D_EF. Then the last valid row samples mu at an offset D_EF + mu_pow_offset, with the PoW operations (if enabled) squeezed in between.

blocks.heading: mu PoW: observe witness, sample, and check ExpBitsLen
blocks.explain: When mu_pow_bits > 0, the verifier requires grinding: observe mu_pow_witness, sample mu_pow_sample, then assert that raising the BabyBear generator to the low mu_pow_bits of the sample yields one — equivalent to saying those low bits are zero. The ExpBitsLenAir lookup table enforces the exponentiation.

blocks.heading: mu-weighted RLC: whir_claim accumulator
blocks.explain: mu_pow chains mu^0 = 1, mu, mu^2, … across valid rows (mu is constant within a proof block). whir_claim accumulates Σ_i mu^i · stacking_claim_i. This is the single extension-field value that WHIR will verify corresponds to an honestly-folded multilinear polynomial.

blocks.heading: Emit to WhirModuleBus and WhirMuBus
blocks.explain: On the last valid row of the block, the phase emits (tidx_whir, whir_claim) on WhirModuleBus — this is what WhirRoundAir consumes to start Phase 4 — and broadcasts mu on WhirMuBus so InitialOpenedValuesAir can re-derive the same linear combination on the WHIR side.

blocks.html:
Recap. Phase 3 is a nested reduction. At the outer level we run a univariate sumcheck round plus n_stack multilinear sumcheck rounds, reducing Σ (col_claim + lambda · rot_claim) · lambda_pow to a single claim s_{n_stack}(u_{n_stack}). At the inner level, that claim is expressed as an inner product of the w_stack stacking openings with coefficients assembled from prism kernel values (from EqBaseAir) and eq-MLE values over row-index bits (from EqBitsAir). Finally, the openings are batched with mu into whir_claim, which Phase 4 (WHIR) verifies via a low-degree test over u_cube = (u_0^1, u_0^2, …, u_0^{2^{l_skip-1}}, u_1, …, u_{n_stack}).

The bus handoff summary: StackingModuleBus and ColumnClaimsBus in; WhirModuleBus, WhirMuBus, WhirOpeningPointBus, and StackingIndicesBus out; sumcheck and eq-kernel plumbing internal.
